promotion_rule: local fits establish package pad identity only; they are not electrical connectivity evidence

anchors.observation: bottom-left ВН59 contact immediately below the left-facing notch
bottom-right contact of the 14-pin row
top-right contact of the opposite row
top-left contact beside the notch end

packages.note: The marking is unambiguous КР580ВН59. This fit replaces global projections displaced onto adjacent resistors and the package body, corrects the source footprint centre to approximately (192.44,110.08) mm through the registered component panorama, and provides physical PIC endpoint geometry for the FDC interrupt audit.

anchors.observation: centre of bottom-left socket contact below the notch
centre of fourth bottom-row socket contact
centre of bottom-right socket contact

packages.note: Corrected horizontal/notch-left posture and physical pin sequence; top row is partly obscured and is deliberately not used as a fit anchor.

anchors.observation: centre of lower-row rightmost soldered socket pad
centre of lower-row middle soldered socket pad
centre of lower-row leftmost soldered socket pad

packages.note: Direct solder-side view is mirrored relative to component side; the similarity fit uses KiCad pad geometry and the photographed lower row.

anchors.observation: centre of top-left socket contact below the notch
centre of fourth left-row socket contact
centre of bottom-left socket contact

packages.note: Physical D2 is a vertical DIP-16 socket; the check anchor is independent of the two endpoint anchors.

anchors.observation: centre of mirrored upper-right solder joint
centre of fourth right-column solder joint
centre of mirrored lower-right solder joint

packages.note: Two vertical 8-pad columns are visible; direct solder view reverses the component-side left/right sequence.

anchors.observation: centre of upper pad on the D4 mirrored input row
independent fifth-pad centre on the same row
centre of lower pad on the D4 mirrored input row

packages.note: Fits the D4 input row independently to identify the five remote D2 solder-trace landings.

anchors.observation: top joint of the mirrored pin-1/14 solder column
independent bottom joint of the same coherent 14-contact column
bottom joint of the opposite 14-contact column
independent top joint of the opposite coherent column

packages.note: The two complete 14-contact columns identify the D11 КР580ВВ51А footprint. This package-local reflected fit replaces whole-board projections displaced about 100 pixels onto adjacent copper and reserves both unused corners for independent validation.

anchors.observation: top-left contact beside the top-facing notch
independent fourth contact on the left package column
bottom-left contact on the same coherent eight-pad column

packages.note: Direct fit to the photographed vertical К555ИЕ7 D106 package. It replaces global projections displaced leftward and onto the body; as with every local fit, it establishes pad identity only, not electrical continuity.

anchors.observation: top joint of the mirrored pin-1/8 solder column
independent fifth joint in the same coherent column
seventh joint near the bottom of the same coherent column

packages.note: Reflected fit to the two photographed 8-pad D106 solder columns. It corrects the package identity and pad locations only; trace destinations remain continuity questions.

anchors.observation: top-left contact beside the top-facing notch on the К155ЛН3 package
independent fourth contact on the unobscured left column
bottom-left ground contact on the same coherent seven-pad column

packages.note: Direct fit to the actual vertical К155ЛН3 D28 immediately right of D106. The black cable obscures much of the opposite column, so only the coherent left column anchors the fit; pad identity is not electrical continuity.

anchors.observation: top joint of the mirrored pin-1/7 solder column
independent fifth joint in the same coherent column
sixth joint near the bottom of the same coherent column

packages.note: Reflected fit to the two photographed 7-pad D28 solder columns immediately beside D106. It identifies package pads only; the traced copper still requires complete remote-pad proof.

anchors.observation: bottom-right contact beside the right-facing notch and pin-1 corner mark
independent fourth contact on the unobscured bottom row
bottom-left contact at the far end of the same coherent eight-pad row

packages.note: Direct fit to the horizontal notch-right К555КП12 D95. The pin-1 corner mark and both complete contact rows correct the prior reversed/offset projections; pad identity is not functional continuity.

anchors.observation: top-left contact beside the top-facing notch on the КМ555ТМ2 package
independent fourth contact on the left package column
bottom-left ground contact on the same coherent seven-pad column

packages.note: Direct fit to the vertical КМ555ТМ2 D96 immediately right of D28. It establishes package identity and relative placement only; functional FDC continuity remains unresolved.

anchors.observation: upper-right contact beside the right-facing package notch
independent fourth contact on the upper row
upper-left contact at the far end of the same coherent row

packages.note: The physical К555ИР16 is horizontal with its notch to the right. This package-local fit replaces global seed projections that landed in the parallel-rail field left of the actual IC.

anchors.observation: leftmost contact on the mirrored upper row
independent fourth contact on the mirrored upper row
rightmost contact on the same coherent row

packages.note: Transferred through the registered component and solder panoramas, then anchored in the original solder image. The fit replaces global projections that landed about 300 pixels to the right of the actual package.

anchors.observation: rightmost contact of the exposed socket upper row; D94 control-fanout end
independent tenth contact on the same exposed socket row
leftmost contact of the exposed socket upper row

packages.note: This close-up was taken with the known КР1818ВГ93 removed from its socket, exposing all forty socket contacts and their component-side fanout. Pin 1 is the upper-row contact nearest D94; the printed 40 at the lower-right socket end independently confirms the row orientation.

anchors.observation: top joint of the mirrored pin-1/20 solder column
independent tenth joint in the same 20-contact column
nineteenth joint near the bottom of the same coherent column

packages.note: Original solder tile exposes the full D93 2x20 footprint. This package-local reflected fit replaces board-level projections displaced roughly 80 pixels from the actual solder joints.

anchors.observation: bottom-left pad immediately below the left-facing notch
independent fifth pad on the lower row
bottom-right pad on the lower row

packages.note: Physical D100 is the horizontal notch-left КР580ВА87 package immediately right of D94; older global projections landed on unrelated packages.

anchors.observation: first unobscured lower-row contact right of the taped package edge
independent middle lower-row contact
last unobscured lower-row contact before the white wire

packages.note: Marked horizontal К155ЛП11 immediately right of D100; outer contacts are wire-obscured, so pins 2/7 anchor the coherent row.

anchors.observation: rightmost joint of the mirrored pin-1/8 row
leftmost joint of the same complete eight-contact row
leftmost joint of the opposite eight-contact row
independent rightmost joint of the opposite coherent row

packages.note: Direct solder-side 2x8 row is mirrored relative to the component package. The affine fit accommodates the photographed perspective and replaces board-level projections displaced roughly 330 pixels to the left.
